notes:
Goal: introduce a backend-agnostic PrintOptions domain object and extend PrinterInterface so jobs can carry options.

Approach:
- New dataclass PrintOptions{copies:int, duplex:Duplex, color:ColorMode, paper_size:PaperSize, number_up:int, page_ranges:str, printer:Optional[str]} with enums Duplex/ColorMode/PaperSize.
- New PrintResult{success:bool, job_id:Optional[str], message:str} and PrinterInfo{name, is_default, info}.
- PrinterInterface.print_file(path, options)->PrintResult; add list_printers()->List[PrinterInfo] and get_job_state(job_id)->JobState.
- SystemPrinter switches from `lpr` to `lp` to capture the job id ("request id is <printer>-<n>"). lp-args translation lives in the adapter (CUPS detail), keeping PrintOptions pure data.
- copies->`-n`, duplex->`-o sides=`, color->`-o ColorModel=Gray`, paper->`-o media=`, number_up->`-o number-up=`, page_ranges->`-o page-ranges=`, printer->`-d`.

Foundation for all UI features. Keep PrintOptions immutable-ish (dataclass with replace()).
title: Print options domain model + CUPS lp adapter

notes:
Debt: adapters build shell strings with f-strings and quoted paths (printerbot.py:108-140,157,172) -> shell-injection surface once printer names / option values flow in from user-controlled settings. os.system also can't capture stdout cleanly (needed for job id + printer list).

Fix: route all external commands through subprocess.run(arg_list, capture_output=True). No shell=True. Add a thin runner so tests patch one seam instead of os.system/os.popen everywhere. Update tests accordingly.

Prereq for pb-domain-options (needs stdout capture for lp job id and lpstat parsing).
title: Replace os.system/os.popen with subprocess arg-lists

notes:
Debt: callback_data embeds the absolute processed file path (printerbot.py:589-590). Telegram caps callback_data at 64 bytes (long temp paths can overflow) and it leaks server filesystem paths to the client. Splitting on space (printerbot.py:615) also breaks on paths with spaces.

Fix: a PendingJobRegistry keyed by a short opaque token stored in context.user_data; callback_data becomes "<action> <token>". Registry holds {file_path, page_count, PrintOptions}. Enables the options panel (needs mutable per-job state) too.
title: Token registry instead of file paths in callback_data

notes:
Debt: authorized_users is an in-memory set (printerbot.py:189,209); every process restart de-authorizes everyone, forcing re-/auth. systemd restarts make this user-visible.

Fix: a PersistentAuthManager backed by a small JSON store (same storage seam as user settings). Keep AuthManagerInterface unchanged; only the storage backing changes. SRP: auth store separate from settings store.
title: Persist authorized users across restarts

notes:
Goal: replace the static Print/Delete card (printerbot.py:588-595) with an interactive options card shown before printing.

UI: inline keyboard reflecting current PrintOptions from the job registry. Rows: copies stepper (➖ N ➕), duplex toggle, color/grayscale toggle, paper-size toggle, pages-per-sheet toggle, page-range (preset/all), then 🖨️ Print / 🗑️ Delete. Each toggle mutates the registry entry and edits the message markup in place (editMessageReplyMarkup via existing _update_message seam).

Keyboard construction must be a pure function PrintOptions->InlineKeyboardMarkup so it is unit-testable without Telegram. Defaults seed from the user's saved settings (pb-settings).
title: Interactive print-options panel (copies/duplex/color/range/paper/n-up)

notes: Goal: show Telegram's `/` autocomplete menu. Call set_my_commands on startup (Application.post_init) with /start,/pending,/completed,/cancel,/settings descriptions. Small, mostly self-contained. Keep the command list a single source of truth reused by help text.
title: Register bot command menu via setMyCommands

notes: Goal: after sending to printer, keep the message updating with CUPS job state (queued->processing->completed) instead of fire-and-forget (printerbot.py:624-630). Needs the job id from pb-domain-options (lp). Poll get_job_state via a bounded async loop / JobQueue; edit message each transition; show 🚫 Cancel wired to cancel_job(job_id) until terminal. Cap polling duration; degrade gracefully if job id unknown.
title: Live job status updates with cancel button

notes: Goal: render page 1 of the processed PDF to a thumbnail and send it with the options card so the user confirms the right file. FileProcessorInterface.render_preview(pdf_path, out_dir)->Optional[str] via `pdftoppm -png -f 1 -l 1 -scale-to 1000`. Send via reply_photo; tolerate render failure (skip preview, still offer print). Clean up preview image after.
title: First-page preview image before printing

notes: Goal: per-user default PrintOptions persisted across restarts, used to seed each job's options. UserSettingsStore interface + JSON-file impl (storage seam shared with pb-td-persist-auth). /settings command opens an inline panel (same widget builder as pb-print-options) that writes defaults. Service exposes get_user_settings/update_user_settings. Default printer also lives here (pb-printer-picker).
title: Persistent per-user default print settings + /settings panel

notes: Goal: when list_printers() returns >1, offer an inline list to choose the target printer (per-job and as a saved default in /settings). Selected printer flows into PrintOptions.printer (-d). If only one printer, hide the picker. Mark default printer in the list.
title: Inline printer picker when multiple CUPS printers exist

notes:
Debt: SubprocessCommandRunner.run is synchronous; it's called from async handlers (print, live-status polling via get_job_state every few seconds, list_printers on every panel render). Each call blocks the single event loop for the subprocess duration. Fine for a single-user home bot, noticeable under concurrency.

Fix: run blocking commands via asyncio.to_thread / loop.run_in_executor, or add an AsyncCommandRunner using asyncio.create_subprocess_exec. The CommandRunner seam already isolates this; only the async call sites change.
title: CUPS subprocess calls block the asyncio event loop

notes:
Debt: PersistentAuthManager and StoreBackedUserSettings both wrap the same JsonFileStore and do load()->mutate->save(). No file lock. Single-process polling bot serializes handlers on one event loop so practically safe today, but a second process (or future threads) could clobber writes. JsonFileStore.save is atomic per-write (tmp+os.replace) but not across the read-modify-write.

Fix options: per-key merge on save, a simple file lock (fcntl), or split into separate files. Low priority while single-process.
title: Auth + settings share one JSON file with unlocked read-modify-write

notes: Debt: printerbot.py is now ~1000 lines spanning domain types, infra (runner/store), adapters, service, UI builders and the bot. Still readable via section banners, but a package (domain.py, adapters.py, service.py, ui.py, bot.py) would localize change and speed tests. Deferred to keep `from printerbot import ...` stable for the existing test suite; do it with a compatibility shim re-exporting names. Low priority.
title: Split printerbot.py into a package as it grows

notes:
Goal: a per-user "dry run" toggle in /settings. When on, printing logs the exact lp command instead of executing it, and reports back that nothing was printed.

Approach:
- Add dry_run:bool=False to PrintOptions (serialized in to_dict/from_dict, seeds each job from settings like other options).
- apply_option_action: "dryrun" verb toggles it.
- build_options_keyboard: show the toggle ONLY in settings scope (it's a mode/preference, not a per-document option); job panel still inherits and honors the value.
- SystemPrinter.print_file: if options.dry_run, build args, log "[DRY RUN] would run: <shlex-quoted cmd>" via an injected logger, and return PrintResult(success=True, job_id=None, ...) WITHOUT calling the runner. job_id None => no live-status polling.
- Service.print_file: dry-run-aware success message.
title: Dry-run toggle in settings: log lp command instead of executing

notes:
Goal: a very detailed review of the whole bot (printerbot.py + tests.py) to catch every plausible bug before calling the feature work done. Use an independent adversarial pass plus a manual read.

Scope/checklist:
- Async correctness: handlers awaiting non-async mocks, create_task lifecycle, edit-on-photo vs text (caption vs text), event-loop blocking.
- Callback grammar: parsing "verb scope:key", tokens with no spaces, 64-byte callback_data limit, expired/missing registry entries.
- Live status polling: pending vs processing vs unknown transitions, terminal detection, cancel wiring, premature "done".
- Options -> lp args translation correctness (sides/media/ColorModel/number-up/page-ranges/copies/-d), default-printer parsing.
- Persistence: JSON read-modify-write, atomic save, enum (de)serialization tolerance, dry_run flag.
- File handling: path validation/traversal, temp filename collisions, preview cleanup, conversion failure cleanup.
- Stale comments / docstrings that no longer match the code.

Output: fix everything found; keep tests green; add regression tests for any real bug.
title: Final detailed code review: find/fix bugs and stale comments

notes: Goal: ensure README accurately reflects the final code after review fixes. Verify every command, option, and dependency named matches the code; document running tests + coverage locally. Badge markdown added by pb-coverage-badge.
title: Update README to final state (commands, options, dry-run, dev/test)

notes: Goal: .github/workflows/ci.yml that on push/PR to main sets up Python, installs deps (python-telegram-bot, pytest, pytest-asyncio, pytest-cov, coverage-badge), runs the suite with coverage (--cov=printerbot), and reports coverage. Pin a Python version matching dev (3.11). Keep it fast and deterministic.
title: GitHub Actions CI: run tests + measure coverage

notes: Goal: generate a coverage badge SVG stored at .github/badges/coverage.svg and display it at the top of README.md. Generate an initial badge locally from real measured coverage (coverage-badge), and have the CI workflow regenerate it on push to main and commit it back if changed (with [skip ci] to avoid loops). Document the source of truth.
title: Coverage badge at .github/badges/coverage.svg shown in README

notes:
Context: review fixed the per-conversion leak and added a startup cleanup_stale_files() sweep (24h TTL). Remaining gap: files for jobs abandoned mid-session (panel never actioned) sit until the next restart's sweep, and the in-memory job registry (context.user_data) is lost on restart so those tokens are orphaned.

Fix options: a periodic JobQueue sweep (e.g. hourly), and/or persist the registry (PicklePersistence) so tokens survive restarts. Low priority — bounded by the startup sweep.
title: Abandoned job files only swept at startup, not during a session

notes:
Done. Independent adversarial pass + manual review. Fixed:
1. Range-input retry: keep awaiting_range on invalid input (was dropped).
2. Temp-file leak: remove original on conversion failure and after non-PDF conversion; replaced _cleanup_file with _remove_quietly.
3. Orphan files: added startup cleanup_stale_files() sweep (24h TTL); remaining mid-session gap filed as pb-td-job-registry-gc.
4. file_size None -> coerce to 0 in _extract_file_info (was TypeError in validate_file).
5. Path containment: os.path.commonpath instead of startswith (sibling-prefix bypass).
6. Markdown injection: fenced_block() neutralizes backticks in CUPS output before fencing.
7. /cancel uses context.args[0] (was ''.join).
8/extra. None-message guards in _prompt_for_range and _start_live_status.
9. generate_temp_filename uses secrets.token_hex (dropped private tempfile API + collision risk); removed unused tempfile import.
Trimmed unused test imports. 97 -> 106 tests. pyflakes clean.

Goal: after ALL other work is done (features, tech-debt, review fixes, README, CI, coverage badge, package split), run one more thorough deep review of the entire package and CI. Independent adversarial pass + manual read. Verify: no regressions from the module split or async/locking changes; CI workflow is correct; coverage badge generation is reproducible; README matches reality; all tests pass; pyflakes clean. Fix anything found.
title: Final deep review after everything is complete

notes: Done. Independent adversarial pass + structural equivalence check (167=167 symbols, no code lost in split). No blockers/majors. Fixed: /completed showed oldest-10 instead of newest-10 (real bug); live-status could sit on "queued…" for 120s if a fast job vanished before being seen active (added UNKNOWN_GRACE_POLLS finalize). Gitignored the flock sidecar (state.json.lock). Left as intentional: small local-FS auth/settings reads on the loop (negligible vs subprocess; offloading tiny reads would add thread churn). 108 tests, pyflakes clean, badge 73%.
